headlines.title: Downing Street hits out at 'people seeking to stir division' after Vance's Nowak post
headlines.description: The US vice-president wrote the "only response is righteous anger" when referring to Henry Nowak's death on X.

headlines.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
headlines.description: The US military says it struck Iranian drones and radar sites. Tehran responds by targeting US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

headlines.title: Tributes to Buffy and Ted Lasso star Anthony Head after death aged 72
headlines.description: The British actor, who also appeared in Merlin and Little Britain, died of complications from pneumonia.

headlines.title: D-Day veterans mark anniversary as nearly 100 British names added to memorial
headlines.description: Tributes are under way to mark the largest military seaborne operation ever attempted.

headlines.title: Armenia braces for election as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
headlines.description: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

headlines.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
headlines.description: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

headlines.title: Singer Duffy to return for first live concert in more than 15 years
headlines.description: The Welsh singer has announced her first concert after a sexual assault led her to step away from public life.

headlines.title: Giant banquets rile radical left in France
headlines.description: The feasting phenomenon run by Le Canon Français has become a hot political issue.

headlines.title: Late night but 'full throttle' - the UK public screenings going all out this World Cup
headlines.description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just around the corner, why not catch a bit of the action from a more surprising location?

headlines.title: Your middle-aged friend starts dating your daughter: Drama explores what happens next
headlines.description: The new drama centres on Alice discovering best friend Steve is dating her 26-year-old daughter.

headlines.title: Rumours and speculation as fans forecast date of Taylor Swift's wedding
headlines.description: Swifties worldwide are looking for clues about the superstar's nuptials to American football star Travis Kelce.

headlines.title: How a virtual space battle lost gamers £400,000
headlines.description: One of the most violent chapters in EVE Online history wiped out an empire that had taken years to build.

headlines.title: Seven spectacular night sky events to watch out for this summer
headlines.description: From a lunar eclipse to one of the best meteor showers of the calendar year, there are a number of impressive sights to behold in the summer night sky.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Flagship breaks down' and 'Beer we go!'
headlines.description: The World Cup features on many of Saturday's front pages - as well as the woes of one of Britain's aircraft carriers.

headlines.title: Six charged with violent disorder after Nowak protest
headlines.description: The six men are remanded in custody and due to appear at Southampton Magistrates' Court later.

headlines.title: Euromillions winner dies in suspected hit-and-run
headlines.description: Anthony Canty was knocked off his bicycle and an 18-year-old has since been arrested, police say.

headlines.title: Man dies after bitten by shark in Western Australia, police say
headlines.description: The man was spearfishing off Michaelmas Island with his family, according to local reports.

headlines.title: Canada bans Texas cattle over flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in US
headlines.description: The ban came as a second infected calf was identified in Texas, and the US state declaring a state of disaster over the outbreak.

headlines.title: Man charged over Barry Island stabbing after boy has leg amputated
headlines.description: An 18-year-old is due in court charged with wounding with intent and possessing a knife.

headlines.title: 'He's causing havoc': Parrot loose in Inverness blamed for damage to cars
headlines.description: Residents of Lochardil say the parakeet is pecking chunks out of parts of their cars.

headlines.title: I moved from Ethiopia to Shetland - and I've brought the coffee with me
headlines.description: Netsanet Sori is bringing a flavour of the farm where she grew up to her new home 4,000 miles away.

headlines.title: Rivals costume designer honoured to make outfit for Dame Jilly Cooper
headlines.description: TV drama Rivals' costume designer says it was an honour creating an outfit for Dame Jilly Cooper.

headlines.title: 'It was an easy decision': Man donates part of his brain to help find cure for dementia
headlines.description: Aidan McAllister gave permission for doctors to take a part of his healthy brain matter to use for dementia research.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Will This Be The Donald Trump World Cup? (With Football Daily, Part 1)
headlines.description: And what’s going on with the Infantino bromance?

headlines.title: Anthropic: AI could escape human control
headlines.description: US firm proposes coordinated global slowdown on building advanced artificial intelligence

headlines.title: Explore a place where science, politics and culture collide
headlines.description: Film exploring the human stories behind mankind’s biggest experiment in particle physics.

headlines.title: Mr Irreplaceable and Ballon d'Or contender - is this Kane's time?
headlines.description: Harry Kane has been described as irreplaceable for England, but will the World Cup provide the stage for him to prove he is now the best?

headlines.title: Teenage star v 500-1 qualifier - a French Open final of contrasts
headlines.description: Mirra Andreeva is a teenage star long predicted to win a Grand Slam title while Maja Chwalinska is a qualifier who came out of nowhere. On Saturday, they meet in the French Open final.

headlines.title: Vaughan feels sorry for batters on poor Lord's pitch
headlines.description: Michael Vaughan says he feels sorry for batters over the state of the Lord's pitch after 33 wickets fell on the opening two days of the first Test between England and New Zealand.

headlines.title: Tuchel will not let Tampa pitch affect selection
headlines.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel says reports about the state of the pitch at the venue for his side's friendly against New Zealand will not influence team selection.

headlines.title: 17 wickets fall as England take control on another action-packed day
headlines.description: England have a golden opportunity for victory in the first Test against New Zealand despite a chaotic collapse of four wickets for one run in 11 balls on the second day at Lord's.

headlines.title: The pioneer in an unlikely World Cup team
headlines.description: When Desmond Armstrong faced the media at the World Cup in 1990, the first question he got was one that would stick in his mind for the wrong reasons.
